w:r.w:t: scale. It strips away enterprise-level complexities like sentiment analysis and multi-node GPU clusters, focusing instead on the core
Project: AI-Driven Automated Attendance System (MVP Prototype)
Core CV Engine, Hit-Based Attendance, and Admin/Faculty Workflows

The system is a web-based portal designed to automate student attendance using Computer Vision. It replaces manual signing with a "Scheduled Hit" system where the camera automatically detects and marks students at specific intervals during a session.
model with a centralized authentication page.
Manage Courses, Clusters, Faculty, Students, Classrooms, and Time Slots. Import data (CSV/PDF).
View assigned courses, trigger AI attendance sessions, verify and override attendance, generate reports.
Admin shall be able to group courses into logical clusters (e.g.,
Admin shall create and remove Faculty and Student profiles.
button must access the live camera to capture the student's face for model training.
—students falling below this are flagged in
Upon login, faculty see only their assigned courses.
When the session starts (e.g., 1:00 PM), the teacher clicks "Start AI Attendance."
The first hit activates automatically at a threshold (e.g., T+20 mins). The camera detects students, marks them, and turns off. The process repeats for the second hit.
At the end of the session, the system displays a full list of enrolled students. Faculty can manually change a status (e.g., from Absent to Present) before clicking
The engine must identify all detected students against the trained dataset.
Students detected in the hits are marked "Present"; all others are defaulted to "Absent."
The system must allow for model training based on the images captured/uploaded by the Admin.
The system must generate three specific report types for Faculty:
Cumulative attendance for all students in a course.
Attendance data filtered by physical classroom location.
A detailed view of a single student's attendance across a specific course's sessions.
or React (based on your proficiency in Python-based UI tools).
for embeddings (high accuracy for identity fidelity).
SQLite or PostgreSQL for storing student metadata and attendance "hits."
The CV engine must maintain 100% accuracy in facial structure recognition, ensuring no changes to student identity during processing.
Authentication is required for all dashboard access; session data must be encrypted.
The "Hit" detection should complete within seconds to ensure the camera does not remain active longer than necessary.

Sentiment analysis is explicitly excluded from this version to focus on the stability of the attendance hits and the accuracy of the recognition engine. The system should prioritize "clean" data entry for the training set (Admin side) to ensure the AI performs well during live sessions.
This table handles the authentication and ensures Admins and Faculty are redirected to their respective dashboards upon login.
The system organizes courses into clusters (e.g.,
(For the CV engine to access the live feed)
This table includes the "Take Picture" requirement for training the AI model.
(Path to the image captured via "Take Picture")
(The 512-d vector extracted for the AI engine)
This is the heart of your MVP. It tracks the 14 weeks, 2 sessions per week, and the 2 threshold hits per session.
